title: Figure 24 in Craniodental anatomy of late Oligocene archaeohyracids (Notoungulata, Mammalia) from Bolivia and Argentina and new phylogenetic hypotheses
author: Billet, Guillaume
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 24. Cladograms showing the phylogenetic relationships of archaeohyracids plus hegetotheriids (plus mesotheriids) from the cladistic analysis (five most parsimonious trees with 66 steps, CI = 0.79, RI = 0.88). A, strict consensus tree (68 steps, CI = 0.76, RI = 0.86). Nodes that have a Bremer support superior to 2 are indicated as '> 2'; all others have a Bremer support equal to 1. B, majority-rule consensus (66 steps, CI = 0.79, RI = 0.88); 60% refers to the percentage of the most parsimonious cladograms that present this node. C, Adams consensus tree (68 steps, CI = 0.76, RI = 0.86).
